The rainfall in a region is modeled by the function $h(x) = x^2 - 2x + 1$. Describe and apply a vertical shift of $2$ units up and a horizontal shift of $1$ unit to the right to the function. Show all work and graphs.

The function $f(x) = x^2$ represents the growth rate of a particular ecosystem. Apply a reflection over the y-axis and a vertical compression by a factor of $2$ to the function. Compare the transformed graph to the parent function and describe any similarities or differences. Show all work and graphs.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of understanding function transformations and their effects on graphs.
Encourage students to use graphing software or sketch graphs by hand to visualize transformations.
Remind students to show all work and explain their reasoning for each problem.
